title: On The Same (On The Upright Judge, Who Condemned The Drapier's Printer) The Judge Speaks
content:
I'm not the grandson of that ass Quin;[1]
    Nor can you prove it, Mr. Pasquin.
    My grandame had gallants by twenties,
    And bore my mother by a 'prentice.
    This when my grandsire knew, they tell us he
    In Christ-Church cut his throat for jealousy.
    And, since the alderman was mad you say,
    Then I must be so too, ex traduce.
meaning:
Jonathan Swift's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On The Same (On The Upright Judge, Who Condemned The Drapier's Printer) The Judge Speaks"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
